comment: 55yo male, stonemason / engineered-quartz fabricator with 25 years documented respirable crystalline silica (RCS) exposure. Established radiographic silicosis on HRCT (small rounded opacities, ILO 1/1). No personal silica-attributable malignancy diagnosis. IARC Group 1 carcinogen (Monograph 100C, 2012) — lung cancer primary endpoint, risk further elevated with clinically-apparent silicosis. Smoking-modified (additive-to-multiplicative). Engine fires RF-OCC-SILICA-PREVENTION → routes to 2-track PreventionPlan: standard = smoking-cessation counseling, risk-adjusted LDCT lung screening (USPSTF-modified for silicosis cohort), pulmonology co-management for silicosis; aggressive = annual LDCT + sputum cytology consideration + earlier-cohort entry. Validates v0.3 occupational batch-2 pilot. Anchored DIS-NSCLC + DIS-SCLC.
